title: Prunus pilosiuscula
intro: Prunus pilosiuscula, also known as the hairy cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Prunus pilosiuscula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Prunus pilosiuscula is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas to promote health and well-being.
taxonomy: Prunus pilosiuscula is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is classified as a shrub or small tree that is native to China and is known to produce small white flowers. Prunus pilosiuscula is further classified within the genus Prunus, which includes other stone fruit species. The exact classification within the Rosaceae family may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be part of the subfamily Amygdaloideae. As a member of the Prunus genus, Prunus pilosiuscula is closely related to other species such as Prunus mume and Prunus avium.
